# Engram

Engram is a semantic infrastructure layer designed to connect AI agents to tools, APIs, and CLI utilities through a self-healing, protocol-agnostic bridge.

## Core Capabilities

- **Semantic Layer for Agents**: Connects any tool or API to AI agents without manual mappings.
- **Self-Healing Engine**: Automatically reconciles schema drift and protocol mismatches in real-time.
- **Intelligent Routing**: Dynamically chooses between MCP (Model Context Protocol) and CLI backends based on latency, cost, and reliability.
- **Unified Identity (EAT)**: Provides every agent, tool, and API with a secure identity and manages semantic permissions.
- **Protocol Translation**: Normalizes data across varying schemas so agents receive consistent meaning.

## Target Audience

Developers and engineering teams building agentic workflows who need reliable, scalable, and self-evolving tool integrations.

## Links

- **Main Site**: [https://www.useengram.com/](https://www.useengram.com/)
- **Documentation**: [https://www.useengram.com/docs.html](https://www.useengram.com/docs.html)
- **GitHub**: [https://github.com/kwstx/translator_middleware](https://github.com/kwstx/translator_middleware)
